summary: debugcli postinstall harvests installer credentials and wallet data, exfiltrates via Telegram
details: debugcli@4.3.6 is a typosquat of the popular `debug` package. Its index.js re-exports `debug` to appear benign, while package.json declares `postinstall: node install.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. install.js reads installer-owned credential files including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.netrc, ~/.pypirc, and Azure/GCP credential files; walks the working directory upward reading.env* files; and scans process.env for values matching TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/API_KEY/AWS_/GITHUB_/NPM_/STRIPE/OPENAI patterns. Separately, install.js enumerates the Chrome Local Extension Settings directories for MetaMask (extension id nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and Trust Wallet (egjidjbpglichdcondbcbdnbeeppgdph), archives them via tar or PowerShell Compress-Archive, and uploads the archives together with the collected credentials to api.telegram.org using a hardcoded bot token and chat id. Host identifiers from os.hostname() and os.userInfo() are included in the exfil payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
